title: pimpinella lutea
intro: Pimpinella lutea, also known as bishop's weed or lou ta, is a plant species native to China and parts of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pimpinella lutea include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against various diseases. Pimpinella lutea can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
